title: Ixeridium alpicola
intro: Ixeridium alpicola, also known as Mountain Ixeris or Alpine Ixeris, is a perennial plant species native to the Himalayan region, belonging to the family Compositae (Asteraceae). Traditionally, the plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which offer potential health benefits for individuals suffering from these conditions. The bioactive constituents of Ixeridium alpicola include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Ixeridium alpicola are decoctions and infusions, which are used to treat respiratory issues and as a natural remedy for fever and cough.
taxonomy: Ixeridium alpicola is classified within the plant family Compositae, also known as Asteraceae. This plant is a member of the genus Ixeridium, which consists of species of flowering plants native to New Zealand. The family Compositae is one of the largest and most diverse families of flowering plants, consisting of over 23,000 species.
